title: Robots in Our Daily Life
passage:
Robots in Our Daily Life

Today, we have many smart machines. We call them robots. They use artificial intelligence, or AI, to think and learn. Some robots work in our homes. For example, there is a small, round robot that cleans the floor. It moves around the rooms by itself and sweeps up dust. This robot saves a lot of time for busy parents. Many people love having this little machine in their living room.

Other robots work in schools and hospitals. In some schools, small robots help children learn English. They can speak, sing songs, and play simple word games with students. The children find these robots very funny and friendly. In hospitals, big robots carry medicine and clean sheets to different rooms. This helps doctors and nurses do their work much faster and spend more time with patients.

Robots are also very useful in big factories. They can make cars and pack heavy boxes very fast. These machines do not get tired. They can work all day and all night without sleeping. They do dangerous jobs so human workers can stay safe and healthy. For example, they can work in very hot rooms or carry very heavy metal parts.

Some robots even go to places where humans cannot go. Scientists send robots to the deep ocean to take photos of strange fish. Other robots travel to space. For example, there are robot cars on Mars. They collect rocks and take pictures of the red planet. These robots help us learn about our universe.

In the future, we will have even more robots. Some scientists are building robots that can cook dinner, wash dishes, or drive cars. Many people like robots because they make our lives easier. However, some people worry because robots might take away some jobs. But for now, robots are just very helpful tools in our daily lives.

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: What does the small, round robot in homes do?
questions.explanation: The first paragraph states: 'For example, there is a small, round robot that cleans the floor.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: Why do robots work in factories?

options.A: Because they want to go to school.
options.B: Because they can make cars and pack boxes fast.
options.C: Because they like to play games.
options.D: Because they need to sleep at night.

questions.explanation: The third paragraph states: 'They can make cars and pack heavy boxes very fast.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: Where do scientists send robots to take photos of strange fish?
questions.explanation: The fourth paragraph states: 'Scientists send robots to the deep ocean to take photos of strange fish.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Small robots in schools help children learn English.
questions.explanation: The second paragraph states: 'In some schools, small robots help children learn English.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Robots in factories get tired after working all day.
questions.explanation: The third paragraph states: 'These machines do not get tired.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Robot cars on Mars collect rocks.
questions.explanation: The fourth paragraph states: 'For example, there are robot cars on Mars. They collect rocks...'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Most people think robots are very expensive.
questions.explanation: The passage does not mention how much robots cost or if people think they are expensive.

questions.instruction: Answer the questions below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: What do robots in hospitals carry to rooms besides clean sheets?
questions.explanation: The second paragraph states: 'In hospitals, big robots carry medicine and clean sheets to different rooms.'

questions.instruction: Answer the questions below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: What planet do robot cars travel to?
questions.explanation: The fourth paragraph states: 'For example, there are robot cars on Mars.'

questions.instruction: Answer the questions below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: What might robots take away, according to some people's worries?
questions.explanation: The final paragraph states: 'However, some people worry because robots might take away some jobs.'

vocabulary.meaning: dry dirt that looks like powder
a building where people make things using machines
space, Earth, and all the planets and stars

tactics:
Hướng dẫn làm bài (Tips for A1-A2)

1. Tìm từ khóa trực tiếp: Với trình độ A1-A2, đáp án thường nằm ngay trong bài đọc mà không bị thay đổi nhiều. Hãy tìm đúng các từ như 'hospital', 'Mars', hoặc 'factory' để xác định đoạn chứa câu trả lời.

2. Lưu ý Not Given: Nếu bạn không tìm thấy thông tin nào nói về việc robot đắt hay rẻ (expensive), hãy chọn ngay NOT GIVEN. Không tự suy luận.

3. Giới hạn số từ: Luôn đọc kỹ yêu cầu 'NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS' để đảm bảo không viết dư từ.
